title: Upsert with placeholders for batch inserts
prompt: Write `MyApp.Blog.bulk_upsert_tags/1` that takes a list of tag name strings and bulk-inserts them as `Tag` records, skipping duplicates on the `slug` unique index. Use `Repo.insert_all/3` with `on_conflict: :nothing`, `conflict_target: :slug`. Generate slugs by downcasing and replacing spaces with dashes. Include `inserted_at`/`updated_at` timestamps in each row (`DateTime.utc_now()`). Write to `lib/my_app/blog.ex`.
tier_rationale: Must remember `insert_all` doesn't auto-set timestamps (unlike `insert/2`). Bypass of changesets means manual slug generation, too.
research_citation: AppSignal: 'Batch inserts bypass validation entirely, meaning data integrity checks must be handled manually before insertion'
